element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns crew roles (navigator, science officer, engineer, communications, doctor, captain) and skill training mechanics
Purchase of armor, shields, lasers, missiles; understanding ablative armor and regenerative shields
Limited starting supply creates early resource pressure; mining minerals becomes necessity
Terrain vehicle deployment for mineral/lifeform collection; gravity danger (>8.0g destroys ship)
Eight space-faring races with distinctive speech patterns; player influences reactions via weapons posture and communication styles
Maneuvering, laser vs missile selection based on range, damage to hull and components
Coordinate pair instant travel saves fuel but disorients low-skill navigators; risk/reward system

element_introduction_order.element: Star system scanning and planetary analysis
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Science officer scans temperature, gravity, chemistry; identifies habitable colony worlds (high income)

element_introduction_order.element: Star flaring and ancient race threat
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Broader plot reveals: ancient being destroying stars; becomes main quest objective

typical_relief_triggers: Successful planetary landing and safe terrain exploration
Friendly alien contact and successful trade
Discovering habitable colony world (profitable goal achieved)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sandbox exploration with escalating tension: early game emphasizes safe resource gathering and crew building, mid-game introduces frequent alien encounters and combat, late game pivots to story-driven star flare mystery with higher stakes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: All 800 planets (270 systems × 0-8 planets per system) are optional; player chooses exploration order; colonization logging enables income but is voluntary
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Continuum flux network creates hidden efficient travel routes for players who discover coordinates; disorientation penalty rewards navigator skill investment

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction: safe Starport hub, unlimited crew hiring/training time, fuel constraint creates manageable pressure, first alien encounters are avoidable or manageable
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation: repeated hostile encounters, resource management becomes tighter, ship upgrades necessary for survival, planet gravity hazards increase
difficulty_curve.late_game: Story climax: star flaring phenomenon becomes critical objective, requires synthesis of all mechanics (exploration, diplomacy, combat, analysis)
notable_spikes: First hostile alien encounter (introduces combat necessity)
Fuel scarcity if player explores inefficiently
Star flare discovery and underlying ancient threat revelation

notable_level_patterns.description: Starport serves as safe home base for crew/ship upgrades; player radiates outward to explore 270 star systems freely, returns for resupply and trading
Orbital approach → Mercator map scanning → landing point selection → terrain vehicle deployment; danger escalates from scan phase through landing gravity checks to ground exploration

Eight distinct species with unique personality and motivations; player encounters range from hostile (combat trigger) to pacifistic (safe interaction) to neutral (trading); no two alien species behave identically
Mine minerals → sell at station or to aliens; find lifeforms → sell; discover colony worlds → log for income; all paths optional, encourages replayability
